body.title: Fable review 2026-07-06 — builder levers, ordering, and the aider question (final Fable session before Enterprise-only)
body.text:
# Fable review — builder, principles, levers, ordering

**Provenance:** claude-fable-5 in the CC.0 seat, 2026-07-06, at Akien's request — last Fable availability. Inputs read: coding-agent-survey-aider-swe-agent note, D-coding-loop-redesign-aider-survey-2026-07-04, D-ds-harvest-mode-coding-loop-2026-07-05, slates 07-03..07-06, agentic_loop.py, architect_editor.py, stuck_ladder.py, prompts/coding.md, fable-consult-coding-loop-2026-07-04.

## Verdict
The builder program is mid-diagnosis and the diagnosis is WORKING: editor proven viable (3/3 on hand-written plan), architect plans after the read-window fix, root causes found via io_corpus, failure converted into product (stuck-corpus). The binding constraint is not direction — it is COST PER ITERATION of the outer experiment loop (~4 min/turn devstral => 30min–5h per live data point => a day per diagnosis cycle).

## Still to steal from aider (survey took the big three; these remain)
1. **Edit-format ladder + whole-file fallback** — aider's hardest lesson: small/quantized models cannot follow strict edit formats; the fix is a SIMPLER contract (return the whole file), not 'try harder'. Our editor gets one format (exact-string Edit) and a bare error string. Ladder: exact-Edit -> on failure re-prompt with error + relevant file window (reflection, capped retries) -> whole-file Write fallback. Was correctly deferred as speculative in the survey; NOW LIVE because the editor is the confirmed bottleneck (07-05 funnel).
2. **Edit-format conformance as per-model metric** — a registry field per (model, format) fed by corpus replay; domain picks format the way the router picks tier. Tier-not-model contract extended to edit dialect.
3. **Commit-per-applied-edit in the throwaway clone** — aider auto-commits every LLM edit. For us: finer resolution for corpus_verdict's later-commit-on-same-paths temporal firewall, plus cheap rollback.
4. **External benchmark slice** — ~5 fixed external tasks (exercism-style) in the funnel decouple 'harness improved' from 'our tickets got easier'. Ground truth nobody in the loop authored.

## Where UU is ahead of aider — double down
aider is STATELESS; every session re-pays full price. The corpus->verdict->prereg->stuck-ladder spine is a RATCHET aider has no analog of. Drop-a-ticket-on-stuck is genuinely novel (aider asks the human; we convert failure into a labeled question). Moat = the system gets cheaper per ticket.

**Compiled-inference MVP hiding in plain sight: THE PLAN IS THE INTERMEDIATE REPRESENTATION.** Editor proven viable given a good plan => the nexus should cache (build-packet fingerprint -> plan -> verdict_strength) triples, and a warm plan-hit skips the architect. T-nexus-write-path-ds-architect-loop is UNDERPRICED — it is the cortical-columns thesis instantiated on the coding loop. First warm plan-hit = first real point on the starve curve.

## Cherny delta (Claude Code philosophy)
CC = thin harness + strong model + complexity in INSPECTABLE TEXT (CLAUDE.md/skills/prompts). UU inverted the model half deliberately (weak model + thick ACI — correct per SWE-agent given free-Hex economics). Watch the other half: AgenticLoop flag sprawl (critic_enabled/aci_mode/plan_mode/history_window/tool_names) already produced one real bug (aci_mode starved the architect). Two moves: (a) flags-to-text where possible; at minimum EVERY FLAG STAMPS ITSELF INTO THE io_corpus RECORD — make it an audit-smell invariant; (b) context hygiene by subagent-shape (explore-in-scratch, only the conclusion persists) — the architect/editor split already does this once; apply inside the architect if it wanders again.

## Skills — tuning not overhaul
1. **Prior-art gate**: the aider survey was the highest-ROI act in 4 months and came after 3 sessions tuning the wrong layer. Add to /sorted for L/HIGH-design-risk: 'Who has already solved this? 30-min survey before the first build ticket.' This is the structural compensation for learning-as-you-go.
2. **Funnel-as-CI**: promote the funnel to a one-command versioned eval (fixed sealed ticket set + external slice, scoreboard appended per run). Every coding-loop change gets a funnel run the way every ticket gets proof-on-close. eval_slice.py provides sealing.
3. **Labeling ergonomics**: every human touch should cost one keystroke and land in the corpus as a label. Scarcest resource is Akien-labeled ground truth, not inference.

## What Akien is overlooking
- Recurring failure mode is WRONG-LAYER DIAGNOSIS UNDER MOMENTUM (3 sessions in-loop pre-survey; system-user detour; sandbox-guard detour), not lack of knowledge. Prior-art gate + replay wind-tunnel make stepping back cheaper than pushing forward.
- One human deep: every 'Akien' gate is a serialization point. Mid-sprint steers ('stop sending those paths in') are high-value labels that survive only as memory notes — route them into the corpus.
- CP4 applies to the operator: savestate/day-close ritual taxes the strongest model (five session-close blocks on 07-05); more of it can be zero-inference script.
- Nothing yet shows devstral-24b is above OR below the floor for architect work, because the qwen3-coder:30b A/B never ran — every architect-side finding carries that unpriced confound.

## THE AIDER-AS-DEVICE QUESTION (Akien 2026-07-06: spin aider up on the 3 slow laptops + Ollama Cloud?)
**Yes, but staged — control arm first, fleet later.**
- **Stage 1 (now): aider as the CONTROL ARM, one instance, one box.** Run the same funnel tickets through aider headless (--message --yes, throwaway clone) with the same models (devstral@Hex, qwen3-coder:30b@Hex, one Ollama Cloud model). Crossed with the DS arms this yields a 2x2 that kills BOTH standing confounds in one experiment: aider+devstral succeeds where DS+devstral fails => harness gap (quantified); both fail => model below floor; qwen arms price the model lever. Cheapest highest-information move available.
- **Stage 2: route aider through the Inference Proxy.** aider speaks OpenAI-compatible API via any base URL — expose the inference device as an OpenAI-compatible endpoint and every aider byte lands in io_corpus (satisfies the Inference-Proxy-only hard rule AND makes aider runs harvest data instead of a black box). This endpoint is valuable regardless: any external tool becomes corpus-instrumented.
- **Stage 3 (gated on Stage 1 results + validation capacity): AiderDevice on the laptops.** BaseDevice/BaseShim wrapper, Granny role=builder, throwaway clone, branch-not-main, closes validated the way Igor closes were (unvalidated builder throughput just moves the queue to CC validation — throughput without validation capacity is queue-shifting, not capacity). Laptops are orchestration shells; inference is Hex or Ollama Cloud, so one-M1 is not a constraint. Watch Ollama Cloud free-tier burn (DS cost-constraint memory).
- **Do NOT fleet-first**: same 'fleet before reliability' instinct Akien already correctly deferred for UU-instances-on-all-boxes. One box, one week, then scale. UU-swarm spin-up remains a SEPARATE decision (aider doesn't remove its blocker — deployment/ops is still human/CC work).

## ORDER OF ATTACK (recommended sequencing)
**Phase 0 — this week, cheap, parallelizable:**
1. Labeling page for stuck-corpus-v2 (S) — unblocks classifier quality half; Akien's pass drops to ~15 min.
2. Funnel-as-CI script (S/M) — needed by everything below; fixed sealed ticket set + scoreboard.
3. The 2x2 experiment: DS x {devstral, qwen3-coder:30b} + aider x {same} on the funnel (M) — kills the model-floor and harness-gap confounds at once. qwen3-coder:30b is registered (models_registry.py:296); as 30B-A3B MoE (~3B active) it is likely FASTER than dense-24B on the same 32GB box and stronger at coding — the latency win alone may 5-10x outer-loop iteration speed, which is the binding constraint.
**Phase 1 — the wall (informed by Phase 0 results):**
4. Edit-format ladder + reflection + whole-fallback for the editor.
5. Classifier: plumbing half on hand fixtures now; quality eval when labels land.
**Phase 2 — the ratchet:**
6. Nexus plan-cache write path (fingerprint -> plan -> verdict) — T-nexus-write-path-ds-architect-loop, underpriced.
7. OpenAI-compatible proxy endpoint + AiderDevice Stage 2/3 if the control arm won.
8. Replay wind-tunnel for HARNESS changes (validate loop fixes against recorded corpus in minutes, not live-run hours).
**Standing:** prior-art gate added to /sorted; every-flag-in-corpus invariant added to audit-smell.

## TICKET DRAFTS (for /sorted + audit-ticket — deliberately NOT filed raw; the audit gates exist for a reason)
**Draft A — T-funnel-2x2-model-harness-confound (M, pri ~0.7):** Build/run the 2x2: {DS harness, aider headless} x {devstral-small-2:24b@Hex, qwen3-coder:30b@Hex} over the sealed funnel ticket set (+ optionally one Ollama Cloud arm). aider arm: pip install aider-chat, headless (--message --yes --no-gitignore), throwaway clone per run (~/dicksimnel pattern, relative paths — REMEMBER the 124553ee contamination lesson), OLLAMA_API_BASE at Hex. Record per arm: plan produced? edits attempted? edits applied? tests green? wall-clock/turn; append to funnel scoreboard. Success = a filled 2x2 with per-cell funnel stages; decision rule pre-registered (prereg.py): aider>DS on same model => harness gap is real, prioritize ladder/ACI; DS-qwen > DS-devstral materially => per-role tiering next; all arms fail => model floor, escalate tier or Ollama Cloud. Proof-lever: the scoreboard artifact + prereg record are the observables; run is observation-shaped => shipped-unproven with named lever is honest if no red-form exists.
**Draft B — T-stuck-corpus-label-page (S, pri ~0.65):** One self-contained HTML page (flat-file, no server; reads a JSON manifest of stuck-corpus-v2 transcripts, or a tiny python generator that inlines them) showing one transcript at a time; keys d=design_stuck c=capability_stuck u=unsure, auto-advance, exports labels.json joinable by ticket_id. Output lands beside the sealed slice (eval_slice read-log discipline respected — labels are NEW artifacts, slice content untouched). Success: Akien labels 31 entries in one sitting; labels.json validates against slice manifest ids. Red form exists: test that exported labels join 1:1 to manifest ids and reject unknown ids.
body.sequencing: Phase0: label-page, funnel-as-CI, 2x2 experiment | Phase1: edit-format ladder, classifier | Phase2: nexus plan-cache, proxy-endpoint+AiderDevice, replay wind-tunnel | Standing: prior-art gate, every-flag-in-corpus invariant
body.aider_verdict: Control arm first (one instance, one box, same funnel, 2x2 with qwen3-coder:30b); then OpenAI-compatible proxy endpoint so aider bytes land in io_corpus; AiderDevice fleet on the 3 laptops ONLY after the control arm wins and close-validation capacity is priced. Laptops are shells — inference is Hex/Ollama Cloud, so one-M1 is not the constraint. Do not fleet-first.
